mnemonics: USERS (Usability, Satisfaction, Error Rate, Retention, Success Rate) - A mnemonic for remembering the five common types of measurement instruments in UI/UX.
keyPoints: Quantitative instruments measure numerical data (e.g., task success rate, time on task).
Qualitative instruments gather descriptive feedback (e.g., user interviews, think-aloud protocols).
The choice of instrument depends on the specific research question and what you need to measure (e.g., usability, satisfaction, engagement).
commonMistakes: Relying on a single type of data; effective measurement requires a mix of quantitative and qualitative methods.
Using an instrument that doesn't align with the research goal, such as using a survey to observe actual user behavior instead of attitudes.
